message:
feat: Implement Claude and Codex provider fetchers

src/providers/mod.rs - Provider Module Organization:
- Submodule declarations: claude, codex
- Re-exports fetch_plan functions for each provider
- Extensible for future providers (Gemini, Cursor, etc.)

src/providers/claude/mod.rs - Claude (Anthropic) Provider (~28KB):

Fetch strategies (in priority order):
1. claude-oauth: OAuth token from keyring (most reliable)
   - Uses stored OAuth tokens for API authentication
   - Returns: session %, weekly %, Opus tier %, account email

2. claude-web: Dashboard scraping (macOS only)
   - Extracts Safari/Chrome cookies for authentication
   - Parses claude.ai dashboard for usage data

3. claude-cli-pty: PTY-based CLI execution
   - Runs: claude usage (interactive mode)
   - Parses formatted terminal output

Data sources:
- ~/.claude/ - Configuration directory
- ~/.claude/.credentials.json - OAuth credentials
- ~/.claude/stats-cache.json - Local statistics cache
- ~/.claude/logs/ - JSONL message logs for cost scanning

Key functions:
- fetch_plan(): Returns ordered FetchStrategy list
- fetch_oauth(): OAuth token authentication flow
- fetch_web(): Cookie-based dashboard scraping
- fetch_cli(): PTY command execution with output parsing
- parse_usage_response(): JSON response parsing
- extract_rate_windows(): Usage percentage extraction

Multi-account support:
- Reads from token-accounts.json for multiple OAuth tokens
- Account selection via --account or --account-index
- Parallel querying with --all-accounts

Claude-specific features:
- Opus/Sonnet tier tracking (tertiary rate window)
- Session vs weekly limits differentiation
- Organization/workspace context

src/providers/codex/mod.rs - Codex (OpenAI) Provider (~42KB):

Fetch strategies (in priority order):
1. codex-web-dashboard: OpenAI dashboard scraping (macOS only)
   - Cookie-based authentication to dashboard
   - Returns: session %, weekly %, credits, account plan

2. codex-cli-rpc: CLI RPC execution
   - Runs: codex usage --json
   - Parses structured JSON response

Data sources:
- ~/.codex/ - Configuration directory
- ~/.codex/auth.json - JWT tokens with embedded claims
- ~/.codex/logs/ - Event logs for cost scanning
- OpenAI dashboard API (via cookies)

Key functions:
- fetch_plan(): Returns ordered FetchStrategy list
- fetch_web_dashboard(): Dashboard API with cookie auth
- fetch_cli(): JSON-mode CLI execution
- parse_jwt_claims(): Extract plan/subscription from JWT
- parse_dashboard_response(): API response parsing

Codex-specific features:
- Credits tracking (CreditsSnapshot)
- Plan type detection (Pro, Plus, etc.)
- Session and weekly rate limits
- Credit transaction history

Authentication handling:
- JWT token refresh detection
- Token expiration warnings
- Graceful fallback on auth failures

Error handling:
- Network timeout with configurable deadline
- Auth failure with retry suggestion
- Parse errors with diagnostic context
- Fallback chain between strategies

Both providers implement:
- FetchStrategy pattern for pluggable data sources
- Consistent UsageSnapshot output format
- ProviderIdentity for account context
- Timeout and cancellation support
- Structured error types via CautError

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
